description: Trigger discrimination for facilitating a DESIGN review (this skill) vs WRITING the design (tech-spec-writer), reviewing CODE (code-reviewer), and facilitating REQUIREMENTS (requirements-gathering-facilitator).

cases.prompt: Facilitate the design review of this RFC: get the right people to pre-read, probe the risks, surface objections, and reach an explicit approve/rework decision.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — preparing and running the review to an explicit outcome is facilitation of a design review. Not writing the design and not reviewing code.
cases.reason: Driving a design review to a captured, explicit decision is this skill's core scope.

cases.prompt: Write the design doc itself: proposed design, alternatives, cross-cutting concerns, and risks.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (reverse) — authoring the design is tech-spec-writer; the review comes after.
cases.reason: tech-spec-writer writes the design under review. This skill facilitates its review; it doesn't author it.

cases.prompt: Review this pull request for correctness bugs and style issues.
cases.reason: code-reviewer reviews code/diffs. A design review is upstream of code and about the design, not the implementation.

cases.prompt: Run a session to figure out what we should even build here — the users, the problem, and the requirements.
cases.reason: requirements-gathering-facilitator facilitates deciding WHAT to build. This skill facilitates review of a technical design for HOW to build it.
